source_file: Policies and Regulations on Computer Systems.pdf
clean_markdown_content:
**PREAMBLE**

The Departmental facilities support our academic activities, and students must strictly adhere to regulations governing their use. The Department reserves the right to revoke such privileges from students who abuse the system or violate the rules.

Students are required to observe and follow university rules and regulations. Rules and regulations may be modified or added from time to time. It is the students’ responsibility to check the changes.
summary: Departmental facilities support academic activities; students must follow regulations.

source_file: Policies and Regulations on Computer Systems.pdf
clean_markdown_content:
**ACCESS PRIVILEGE**

Computer Science students with a valid student ID card have access to the Department facilities and laboratories during the opening hours. However, some computer equipment, network, and accounts are restricted to specific courses. Access to such facilities will be terminated two weeks after the semester. Other general-purpose services will normally expire two weeks after students leave the Department due to graduation or withdrawal from the University.

Non-Computer Science students may be allowed to access selected departmental facilities for a definite time period when they are enrolled in Computer Science courses.
summary: Access to facilities is limited to valid student ID holders and specific courses.

source_file: Policies and Regulations on Computer Systems.pdf
category: Regulations and Conditions Governing Computer Usage
clean_markdown_content:
**REGULATIONS AND CONDITIONS GOVERNING COMPUTER USAGE**  

**GENERAL RULES**

1. Computer facilities are dedicated for University activities. Commercial use is strictly prohibited.
2. All users should follow the instructions as announced from time to time.  The Department has the authority to expel those who behave improperly or misuse the facilities.
summary: Computer facilities are for University activities only; commercial use is prohibited.

source_file: Policies and Regulations on Computer Systems.pdf
clean_markdown_content:
**RULES FOR COMPUTER ACCOUNTS**

1. Each user account is for his/her exclusive use. The user is strictly prohibited from giving his/her account access to any other persons. They are also prohibited from using the accounts of any others.
2. Students are responsible for anything happening from their accounts, such as personal view broadcast on public forum or email that may harm others’ feelings.
3. Students are prohibited from breaking into any computer systems.
4. Students are not allowed to broadcast fake, chain or flooding electronic mail. Obscene, pirated or illegal materials in any format (including audio or video) are prohibited.
5. Students are required to observe and follow the updated rules posted in each laboratory.
summary: User accounts must be exclusive; sharing and misuse are prohibited.

source_file: Policies and Regulations on Computer Systems.pdf
clean_markdown_content:
**RULES FOR DEPARTMENTAL FACILITIES**

1. Departmental laboratories open from 8:30 a.m. to 10:30 p.m. weekdays, weekends and public holidays. Students are not allowed to stay in any laboratories outside normal opening hours without permission from the Department.
2. Only authorized students are allowed to enter the laboratories.
3. Quietness, tidiness and cleanliness must be maintained. Any activities, which cause disturbance to other users, are prohibited.
4. Eating, drinking, smoking, gambling and any kind of games inside the laboratories are prohibited.
5. Any kinds of misusing computer systems are prohibited (e.g., locking of the computer without permission).
6. Any kinds of unauthorized attempt access to computer systems are prohibited.
7. Students may be required to present their University identification card upon request by University staff member or student helper on-duty in the laboratories.
8. Students are not allowed to install any software and hardware in departmental facilities without permission. Otherwise, students are responsible for any consequences arising from them. These software and hardware will be removed when found.
9. Students shall not copy in any form the software provided, without the specific approval from the Head of Computer Science Department. Most of the software products are proprietary and are protected by copyright. Students should be aware of their full responsibilities for all licensing requirements of software products that they may have brought along to use on University computing facilities.
10. Students are required to observe the intellectual property and copyright laws for audio and video materials. Students are responsible for any consequences arising from illegal uploading and downloading of the copyright-protected audio and video files.
11. Relocating or tampering with equipment is strictly prohibited. Students are not allowed to unlock and open computer cases/compartments to remove any parts or the Department labels without permission.
12. All manuals and documents are for reference in the laboratories only. Computer manuals can be checked out from the department according to posted procedures.
13. Students shall take steps to avoid wastage and shall be responsible for the replacement cost for any loss or abnormal damage to any media, documentation or equipment due to their misuse.
14. Students should take care of their own belongings. The Department is not responsible for any personal belongings.
summary: Lab access requires adherence to strict rules, including time and software usage.

source_file: Policies and Regulations on Computer Systems.pdf
clean_markdown_content:
**STUDENT PRINT QUOTA**

1. The Department has endorsed providing the following print quotas to:
   
   | Student Year | Yearly quota | Initial Increment | Cumulative Maximum |
   |--------------|--------------|------------------|-------------------|
   | Year 1*      | 400 pages    | pages/week 250   | pages             |
   | Year 2      | 400 pages    | pages/week 250   | pages             |
   | Year 3      | 500 pages    | pages/week 300   | pages             |
   | Year 4      | 600 pages    | pages/week 400   | pages             |
   | MSc         | 600 pages    | pages/week 400   | pages             |

   *Applicable to BCDA students only.

   At the end of each academic year, the print quota balance for Computer Science students will be carried forward to the next academic year. All remaining print quota will expire on 31 August of their graduation year.

  b) Every non-major student taking COMP/IT course(s) will have 100 pages each semester. The print quota balance for every non-major student will NOT be carried forward and will expire at the end of each semester.

2. The print quota provided by the Department is solely for academic purposes. Students are therefore requested to refrain from printing materials not related to their studies. Getting blank papers from the printers through printing is prohibited.
3. Students may extend the print quota at $30 per 100 sheets. Application can be submitted at the general office (DLB626) of the David C. Lam Building in Shaw Campus. Normally, the request will be fulfilled within one working day after the purchase application is received.
4. When students complete or withdraw their studies, their print quota will be automatically forfeited. No claim or refund on the remaining print quota will be entertained.
summary: Print quotas vary by student year; usage restricted to academic purposes.

source_file: Policies and Regulations on Computer Systems.pdf
clean_markdown_content:
**PENALTY**

Any student who violates Clause 1 under Section “Rules for Computer Accounts” or Clause 4 or 5 under Section “Rules for Departmental Facilities” will have the following penalty:

- For first violation, the student concerned cannot use the print service provided by the Department for two weeks, he/she can only login into two computers in each of the three teaching laboratories (FSC801C, FSC801D, RRS638) for two weeks, and his/her name as well as the date and type of violation will be officially recorded.
- For each subsequent violation, the student concerned cannot use the print service provided by the Department for four weeks, he/she can only login into two computers in each of the three teaching laboratories (FSC801C, FSC801D, RRS638) for four weeks, 200 pages of print quota will be deducted and his/her name as well as the date and type of violation will be officially recorded.
- If a student violates the above Clause two or more times, he/she needs to meet the Year Tutor / Programme Coordinator/Director / Department Head.
- If a student violates the above Clause seriously, his/her case will be reported to the Student Affairs Office (SAO). SAO will impose further penalties on the student concerned.
summary: Penalties for rule violations include loss of print services and access.

source_file: Policies and Regulations on Computer Systems.pdf
clean_markdown_content:
**CCTV SURVEILLANCE**

Student Laboratories are under 24 hours CCTV surveillance. Video and audio will be recorded for security and resource management purposes. The recorded data will be processed in accordance with Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance.
summary: 24-hour CCTV surveillance for security and management in student laboratories.

source_file: Policies and Regulations on Computer Systems.pdf
clean_markdown_content:
**SAFETY MEASURES**

1. First aid boxes and fire extinguishers are located near the entrance of laboratories.
2. Emergency phone numbers: Please dial “9” first to reach an outside line.
   - Fire, Ambulance, Police: 999
   - Security Office (24 hours): Campus 3411 7777
3. Please go to Queen Elizabeth or Kwong Wah Hospital for emergency medical treatment. The Baptist Hospital does not offer emergency service.
summary: Safety equipment and emergency contacts for laboratory incidents.
